fields.description: Helm chart version to install. Pin this to prevent auto-upgrades on operator restart. Defaults to the latest chart version bundled with the operator.
If true, the operator creates a FluxCD HelmRelease instead of reconciling the chart directly. This is the legacy v0.x path; v2.x uses the built-in Go reconciler.
Number of Redpanda broker pods. Must be odd for proper Raft quorum (1, 3, or 5). All pods must be Running+Ready before a rolling update proceeds to the next pod.
StatefulSet update strategy. RollingUpdate replaces pods one at a time in reverse ordinal order.
Pod anti-affinity strategy for scheduling brokers on different nodes. 'hard' prevents scheduling on the same node; 'soft' prefers different nodes but allows co-location.
Size of the data PVC per broker pod. Uses Kubernetes resource quantity notation (e.g. '20Gi'). Cannot be shrunk after creation — expansion requires the StorageClass to support it.
Name of the Kubernetes StorageClass used to provision data PVCs. Must support ReadWriteOnce access mode. Leave empty to use the cluster default StorageClass.
CPU cores per broker. Maps to both the container resource request/limit and the --smp flag passed to the Redpanda process (controls how many CPU cores Redpanda uses for its reactor threads).
Maximum memory for the broker container (Kubernetes limit). The Redpanda process gets slightly less; the remainder is reserved for OS page cache which Redpanda relies on for read performance.
Memory explicitly allocated to the Redpanda process via --memory. If unset, the operator calculates this from container.max minus a reserved headroom for page cache (typically 20-25%).
Memory reserved for the OS and non-Redpanda processes within the container. Subtracted from container.max to derive the Redpanda process allocation.
Enable SASL authentication on all Kafka listeners. When true, clients must authenticate using SCRAM credentials. Use with spec.clusterSpec.tls.enabled for encrypted + authenticated connections.
Enable TLS on all listeners cluster-wide. Individual listener TLS settings can override this. Requires TLS certificates (auto-generated by cert-manager or provided via Secret).
Internal Kafka API listener port. Default 9093 when TLS is enabled, 9092 for plaintext. Used for in-cluster Kafka clients.
Enable TLS on the Kafka listener. Overrides the cluster-wide tls.enabled setting for this specific listener.
Require mutual TLS (mTLS) on the Kafka listener — clients must present a valid certificate signed by the configured CA.
Redpanda Admin HTTP API port. Used by rpk, the post-install Helm hook Job, and the configWatcher sidecar to apply cluster config via PUT /v1/cluster_config.
PandaProxy HTTP listener port (REST proxy for Kafka). Allows Kafka produce/consume via plain HTTP without a native Kafka client.
Schema Registry listener port. Implements the Confluent Schema Registry API for Avro, Protobuf, and JSON schema management.
Internal RPC port for broker-to-broker communication (Raft replication, partition leadership, group membership). Never exposed outside the cluster.
Enable external access from outside the Kubernetes cluster. Creates per-broker Services with stable advertised addresses.
External access mechanism. NodePort exposes brokers on random node ports; LoadBalancer provisions a cloud load balancer per broker (one LB per broker, not per cluster).
DNS domain used to construct per-broker advertised addresses for external access. Each broker gets a subdomain: redpanda-{n}.{domain}.
Container image repository for Redpanda brokers. Change this to use a private registry mirror.
Redpanda container image tag. Pin to a specific version for reproducible deployments. The operator validates that this version is compatible with the cluster.

Cluster-level configuration properties applied live via the Admin API (PUT /v1/cluster_config). Changes here do NOT require a pod restart — the configWatcher sidecar detects CR changes and re-applies them. See Redpanda docs for full list of cluster properties.

Tunable configuration properties applied live via the Admin API. These are expert-level settings affecting performance and durability (log segment sizes, compaction intervals, etc.). Wrong values can severely impact performance.

Enable rack awareness for Raft partition placement. Ensures partition replicas are distributed across different failure domains using the topology.kubernetes.io/zone node label. Requires at least 3 different zones.
Redpanda process log level. warn is recommended for production — debug generates very high volume.
Create a Prometheus ServiceMonitor for Redpanda metrics scraping. Requires the prometheus-operator CRD to be installed.
